title: aratitiyopea lopezii var. lopezii
intro: Aratitiyopea lopezii var. lopezii is a plant species in the family Goodeniaceae, commonly known as the 'Ngaiire' or 'Ngaiire plant'. It is native to New Zealand and traditionally used by the indigenous Maori people for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various skin conditions. The Maori people traditionally prepare the plant by making teas, infusions, and poultices, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including wounds, burns, and skin irritations.
